sessionAgent.bannerStaleOne: {{name}} is no longer linked to this machine's agent — messages may not be processed
sessionAgent.bannerOfflineOne: {{name}} is offline — messages may not be processed by the agent
sessionAgent.bannerOfflineMany: {{count}} agents are offline — messages may not be processed
sessionAgent.bannerStaleMany: {{count}} agents need to be rebound to this machine — messages may not be processed
sessionAgent.bannerMixed: {{offlineCount}} agent(s) offline and {{staleCount}} need rebinding — messages may not be processed
sessionAgent.dropdownStale: Agent must be rebound to this machine's identity
sessionAgent.dropdownOffline: Agent is offline — models will be available when it comes online
sessionAgent.sendConfirmBody: Your message will be added to the session. The agent may process it after coming online, but delivery is not guaranteed.
sessionAgent.sendConfirmDismiss: Don't ask again in this session

sessionNotice.sentStaleOne: Sent. {{name}} is no longer linked to this machine's agent — consider @-mentioning the local agent again.
sessionNotice.sentStaleMany: Sent. {{count}} agents need to be rebound.
sessionNotice.sentOfflineOne: Sent. {{name}} is offline; delivery will be attempted when online (not guaranteed).
sessionNotice.sentOfflineMany: Sent. {{count}} agents are offline; delivery will be attempted when online (not guaranteed).
sessionNotice.sentMixed: Sent. {{offlineCount}} agent(s) offline and {{staleCount}} need rebinding — check delivery when they are available.

workspace.createWorkspaceDesc: Pick a path and create a new empty workspace.
workspace.startupPromptTitle: Choose a workspace to get started
workspace.startupPromptBody: Please choose an existing workspace or create a new one before continuing.
workspace.startupPromptTip: If your last workspace still exists, TeamClaw will open it automatically next time.
workspace.startupFeatureFastDesc: If your last workspace is still available, the app will reopen it automatically next time.
workspace.startupFeatureSafeDesc: Choose an existing folder if you already have a project, or create a clean workspace for a new one.
workspace.selectExistingDesc: Open an existing project or directory.
workspace.webModeBody: Running in web mode. Enter a workspace path so TeamClaw knows where to read and write project files.
workspace.webModeTipBody: Use an absolute path for the smoothest setup, especially when the server runs in a different environment.
workspace.webModeAccessBody: Make sure the agent runtime has permission to access this directory before continuing.

prompt.needShareInitTitle: Finish share directory setup in team settings
prompt.needShareInitDesc: This workspace does not have teamclaw-team/ yet. Go to Settings → Team to finish setup.
prompt.teamMismatchConfirm:
This workspace is already bound to team "{{teamName}}", which does not match the currently signed-in team "{{currentTeamName}}".

Continuing will delete the teamclaw-team/ directory in this workspace and re-initialize it for the current team (other files are left untouched).

Continue?
prompt.teamMismatchConfirmAgain:
Confirm again: this will delete the teamclaw-team/ directory in the workspace (only this one directory). This action cannot be undone.

Confirm deletion?
prompt.oldTeamRepoClearedDesc: Go to Settings → Team to finish share directory setup for the current team.

knowledge.title: Knowledge Base
knowledge.selectWorkspace: Please select a workspace directory first
knowledge.deleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete the following items?
knowledge.fileOpenedDesc: The file is now open in the editor
knowledge.empty: No documents yet. Upload some files to get started.
knowledge.newFolderDesc: Enter a name for the new folder

autoInject.bannerDesc: Automatically retrieve knowledge base and inject relevant content into system prompt before each conversation. No need for the Agent to call tools manually.
autoInject.enableDesc: Automatically retrieve knowledge base before each conversation
autoInject.thresholdDesc: Only inject chunks with similarity above this threshold (0-1), recommended 0.4. If no chunks meet the threshold, nothing will be injected.
autoInject.tip1: Auto inject runs silently before each conversation without showing the retrieval process
autoInject.tip2: The Agent can still manually call knowledge_search for deeper retrieval
autoInject.tip3: Adjusting the similarity threshold controls injection accuracy: higher is more precise but may miss relevant content
autoInject.tip4: Configuration is saved in {{teamclawDir}}/rag-config.json and can be synced across devices

search.hybridWeightDesc: Weight of semantic search in hybrid mode (0-1), higher values rely more on semantic similarity
search.chunkOverlapDesc: Overlap characters between adjacent chunks to avoid losing boundary information

advanced.autoIndexDesc: Automatically index the knowledge/ directory on startup
advanced.fileWatcherDesc: Automatically detect file changes and update incrementally
advanced.rerankEnabledDesc: Use Rerank API to further optimize search results
advanced.rerankTopKDesc: Number of initial results to retrieve before reranking

knowledge.needsReindex: Configuration changes detected (embedding model or chunking parameters). A full re-index is required for changes to take effect.

daemonGeneral.title: General
daemonGeneral.description: Maintain this machine daemon agent and access
daemonGeneral.teamMismatchTitle: Local daemon doesn't match the current team
daemonGeneral.teamMismatchDesc: The daemon is bound to a different team than the one currently selected in the app. Team-shared content syncs and symlinks to the daemon's team, not the current team. To have this machine join the current team's shares, re-initialize the daemon with an invite from the current team.
daemonGeneral.noTeam: Join or create a team before configuring daemon agent settings.
daemonGeneral.noAgent: No daemon agent is associated with this machine yet.

daemonGeneral.backendHint: Click a type to make it the default

daemonGeneral.ownerOnly: Only the agent owner can edit profile and access settings.
daemonGeneral.accessDesc: Rows are read from agent_member_access for this daemon agent.

email.connectDesc: This wizard will guide you through setting up email as a communication channel. The bot will monitor your inbox, process incoming emails through AI, and send threaded replies.
email.senderFilteringDesc: Control which emails get AI responses
email.gmailOAuthDesc: Secure OAuth2 authentication. Requires a Google Cloud project with Gmail API enabled. Best for Gmail accounts with 2FA.
email.imapSmtpDesc: Works with any email provider (Outlook, Yahoo, QQ, etc.). Uses standard username/password or app password authentication.
email.gmailAuthUrlHint: Open this authorization URL in your browser, then paste the returned code.

email.clickFinish: Click "Finish Setup" to save and close.

team.title: Team Shared
team.description: Configure the team shared Git repository, local shared directory, and sync status
team.hostLlmPaneDesc: Configure the shared AI model proxy URL and model list for your team — every member can use them directly.
team.disconnectConfirm: Are you sure you want to disconnect the team shared directory? The {{teamRepoDir}} directory and all its content will be permanently deleted.
team.disconnectSuccessHint: Cloud binding cleared. You can choose OSS, managed Git, or self-hosted Git again.
team.disconnectMigrationRequired: Cloud database is missing the disable_team_share migration. Apply services/supabase/migrations/20260604120000_disable_team_share.sql and redeploy FC.
team.disconnectSchemaDrift: Cloud API schema is out of date (disable_team_share). Deploy the latest FC and Supabase migration, then retry.
team.daemonTeamMismatch: Local daemon is bound to team {{daemonTeamId}}, but you are signed in as {{currentTeamId}}. Re-bind the daemon to the current team in settings, then sync again.
team.desktopOnly: Team shared directory actions require the desktop app
team.disconnectDesc: This will delete local team data ({{teamclawDir}} and {{teamRepoDir}} directories). This action cannot be undone.

repoGuide.title: How to set up a team repository
repoGuide.intro: A shared repository for your team to centrally manage Agent Skills, MCP configurations, and knowledge documents. Use the structure below so TeamClaw can sync correctly.
repoGuide.structureTree:
.
├── .agent/skills/   # Agent Skills — shared AI skill definitions
├── .mcp/            # MCP Configs — shared MCP Server configurations
├── knowledge/       # Knowledge Base — shared docs, specs, and references
├── .gitignore       # Whitelist mode, only tracks the above three directories
└── README.md
repoGuide.dirSkills: Shared Agent Skill definitions (SKILL.md). Skills give AI Agents domain-specific capabilities and guidelines. Team members can reference these in IDEs like Cursor.
repoGuide.dirMcp: Shared MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server config files. Centrally manage MCP tools used by the team (e.g. Playwright, database connectors) for a consistent dev environment.
repoGuide.dirKnowledge: Shared knowledge documents: project conventions, technical proposals, troubleshooting guides, FAQs, and best practices.
repoGuide.usage1: Clone the repository into your workspace. In TeamClaw, connect using the repo URL; the app will clone it into a {{teamRepoDir}} folder in your workspace.
repoGuide.usage2: The repository uses a whitelist .gitignore — everything is ignored by default, only the three shared directories are tracked. You can keep personal files locally without accidental commits.
repoGuide.usage3: In Cursor, use @ to reference Skills and Knowledge from this repository when working with AI.
repoGuide.contributingSkills: To add a Skill: create a subdirectory under .agent/skills/ with a SKILL.md file.
repoGuide.contributingMcp: To add an MCP config: create a <server-name>.json file under .mcp/.
repoGuide.contributingKnowledge: To add knowledge: place Markdown (or other) files in knowledge/.
repoGuide.contributingSecurity: Ensure no sensitive data (keys, credentials) is committed.

team.llmApiKeyHint: API key is read from env var

git.title: Git Repositories
git.description: Configure personal and team git repositories for skills and documents

envVars.title: Environment Variables
envVars.sectionDescription: Securely store API keys, passwords, and other secrets in your system keychain
envVars.addDescription: Add a new secret that will be stored securely in your system keychain.
envVars.editDescription: Update the value for this environment variable. Values are stored securely in your system keychain.
envVars.deleteDescription: Are you sure you want to delete "{{key}}"? This will remove the secret from your system keychain and cannot be undone.
envVars.descriptionPlaceholder: e.g. OpenAI API key for production
envVars.emptyDescription: Store your API keys and passwords securely. Values are encrypted using your system keychain (macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager).
envVars.hintBody: Values are stored in your operating system's native keychain, not in config files. Use ${KEY_NAME} syntax in MCP server environment variables or other configs to reference these secrets.

error.invalidKey: Key must contain only letters, digits, and underscores
error.invalidKeyShared: Shared key must be lowercase letters, digits, underscores (max 64 chars)

envVars.hintTeam: Team variables are encrypted and synced to all team members. They cannot be viewed after saving.
envVars.shareHint: This variable will be encrypted and synced to all team members.

tokenUsage.title: Token Usage

tokenUsage.noData: No stats available. Stats will be created automatically when you start working.
tokenUsage.statsBanner: Data is read from {{teamclawDir}}/stats.json in your workspace. Stats update automatically as you work.

privacy.title: Privacy & Telemetry
privacy.description: Control anonymous usage data collection to help improve TeamClaw.
privacy.analyticsDesc: Store anonymous usage metrics locally (tokens, tool stats, scores). No code, conversations, or personal data. Data stays on your device.

voice.title: Offline Voice Input
voice.description: Download a Whisper model for offline speech-to-text. Smaller models are faster and use less disk space; larger models give better accuracy.
voice.webHint: Voice model settings are only available in the desktop app.
voice.enableVoiceDesc: Show the voice input button and allow voice shortcuts.

leaderboard.title: Team Leaderboard
leaderboard.noData: No leaderboard data yet. Your stats will be automatically synced when you complete sessions or provide feedback.

cache.title: Local Cache
cache.description: Manage the local database that makes screens load instantly. Data is synced from the server in the background.
cache.refreshAllDescription: Clears the local cache for this team and re-downloads everything from the server. Useful if data looks stale.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Customize the system prompt and conversation behavior
prompt.systemPromptPlaceholder: You are a helpful AI assistant that specializes in...
prompt.systemPromptHint: This prompt will be prepended to all conversations to guide the AI's behavior.
prompt.proTipDesc: A good system prompt includes your preferred response style, any domain expertise needed, and specific guidelines for the AI to follow.
prompt.saveReloadWarning: System prompt saved, but Agent runtime reload failed

roles.title: Roles
roles.description: Workspace roles that progressively expose role-specific skills
roles.descriptionDetail: Workspace roles stored in .opencode/roles and loaded by the role plugin
roles.selectWorkspace: Please select a workspace directory first
roles.noRolesHint: Create your first role to group role-specific instructions and skills
roles.attachRequiresName: Set a role name before attaching skills
roles.roleExists: A role with this name already exists
roles.dialogDescription: Edit ROLE.md with structured fields or raw Markdown. Role skills are exposed only after role_load.
roles.name: Role Name
roles.nameHint: Use a stable slug-style identifier. This becomes the role folder name and routing key.
roles.descriptionHint: This is the short summary shown in available_roles, so make it specific and discriminative.
roles.identityHint: Define the role name that will be routed by the model, and the short description exposed during progressive disclosure.
roles.roleSectionHint: Describe the role’s core mandate, expertise boundary, and what it is responsible for in the workspace.
roles.whenToUsePlaceholder: List the situations where this role should be selected
roles.whenToUseHint: List the request patterns, files, or task shapes that should trigger this role instead of another one.
roles.workingStylePlaceholder: Describe how this role should operate
roles.workingStyleHint: Capture behavioral guidelines, review style, priorities, and how the role should communicate or make tradeoffs.
roles.availableRoleSkillsHint: Attach local workspace skills and choose whether to copy or migrate them into .opencode/roles/skills
roles.noAttachableSkills: No local workspace skills are available to attach
roles.deleteConfirm: Delete "{{name}}"? Role skills under .opencode/roles/skills are preserved.

rolesSkills.title: Roles & Skills
rolesSkills.subtitle: Roles define routing and responsibility. Skills provide reusable execution procedures.
rolesSkills.relationHint: Manage routing roles and reusable skills together in one workspace console.
rolesSkills.loadFailed: Failed to load roles and skills

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: Custom AI skills for your workspace
skills.selectWorkspace: Please select a workspace directory first
skills.noSkillsHint: Create your first skill to enhance AI capabilities
skills.dialogDescription: Skills are SKILL.md files with YAML frontmatter. Saved to .opencode/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (OpenCode format).
skills.name: Skill Name
skills.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
skills.restartToLoadNewSkills: New or updated skills were detected. Please wait until skill generation is complete before restarting OpenCode.
skills.restartToApply: Restart OpenCode to apply the new skill permission configuration.
skills.marketplaceSourceHint: Choose a source and install workspace-ready skills.
skills.defaultPermissionHint: Controls the wildcard (*) rule for all skills without a specific override
skills.importZipDescription: Upload a .zip that contains exactly one SKILL.md. The entire skill folder is copied to your skills directory. Archives without SKILL.md are rejected.
skills.importZipHint: Folder name comes from the parent directory of SKILL.md, or from the zip file name if SKILL.md is at the archive root.
skills.detachBeforeDeleteDetailed: This skill is still linked to one or more roles. Remove it from those roles before deleting.
skills.detachBeforeDelete: This skill is linked to one or more roles. Detach it from those roles before deleting.

cron.automationDesc: Schedule recurring tasks for your AI agent. Jobs run automatically and can deliver results through configured channels.

cron.useWorktreeDesc: Execute in a temporary git worktree copy
cron.modelOverrideHint: Select a model or use workspace default.
cron.scopeGlobalHint: Runs in the Daemon default workspace, independent of the active window.
cron.scopeWorkspaceHint: Runs in the selected workspace, supports git worktree isolation.
cron.globalJobNote: Global tasks use the model configuration from the Daemon default workspace.
